overview.title: ShadowRay Cyberattack on Transportation Systems
overview.summary: The ShadowRay campaign targets a major transportation system, employing a sophisticated multi-stage attack leveraging vulnerabilities, persistence mechanisms, and data exfiltration to disrupt operations and steal sensitive information.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt transportation operations and steal sensitive data for financial gain.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather OSINT to identify potential entry points and system architecture, focusing on public domain information related to the transportation network.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop a custom exploit targeting a known vulnerability (CVE-2023-48022) in the Ray AI framework used by the transportation system.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 180 Name: Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels
stage.description: The attackers exploit CVE-2020-5902 to gain unauthorized access to the transportation system's network, initially compromising a web-facing application.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 445 Name: Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers deploy malware to establish a persistent foothold, allowing them to execute arbitrary commands and escalate privileges.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A backdoor is installed to maintain long-term access, ensuring the attackers can return even after system reboots or updates.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 678 Name: System Build Data Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attackers exploit misconfigurations to escalate privileges, gaining access to higher-level system functions and sensitive data.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 81 Name: Web Server Logs Tampering
stage.description: Logs are deleted or manipulated to remove evidence of the attack, hindering incident response and forensic analysis.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226 Name: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Sensitive credentials are extracted, enabling lateral movement within the network and access to restricted areas.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The network is mapped to identify all connected devices and services, facilitating further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 325 Name: TCP Congestion Control Flag (ECN) Probe
stage.description: Lateral movement occurs using valid credentials, accessing critical nodes and expanding the scope of the breach.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Sensitive operational data is collected, potentially impacting the transportation system's ability to function normally.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609 Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: Stolen data is transferred securely to an external server, avoiding detection by using encrypted channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attack culminates in the disruption of transportation services, causing chaos and significant financial loss.

Scenario.summary: The ShadowRay attack demonstrates a highly coordinated and sophisticated cyber operation, exploiting multiple layers of the transportation system's infrastructure to achieve both data theft and operational disruption. The attackers meticulously planned each phase, ensuring persistence, evading detection, and maximizing the impact of their actions.
